topics: General principles for location and layout of shipyards: Functional and geographic aspects, transport, supply industry and human resource
Evolution of first to fourth generation shipyards and their typical layouts
Product and process oriented work breakdown structure: Integrated hull construction, outfitting and painting (IHOP), grouping and work breakdown structure
Zone construction method for hull blocks, outfitting and painting, pipe piece family manufacturing
Material handling, storage and transport systems
Material preparation: Straightening of plates, sand blasting, painting, lofting, nesting
Production management: Pre-production, production and post production stages
Planning, design, production, material management
Quality control/quality assurance, accuracy control, risk management
Project monitoring: Build strategy, progress monitoring, PERT charts
Cutting processes and tools: Mechanical cutting, thermal cutting, general description of the various machines, photoelectric and NC-control devices
Bending of plates and sections, heat-line bending
Assembly of ship's structure: Assembly jigs, structural arrangement of the fore and aft end structure, superstructures, machinery foundation, cranes and davits
Welding in shipbuilding: Welding methods, standards, symbols

Erection of ship's hull: Handling of pre-assembled units in the erection area, different methods of hull assembly, block erection methods (floor, ring and pyramid), single/multi point erection methods
Technological processes in the installation of main machinery, shafting and propeller
Weld defects, distortions and their preventive measures
Testing: Destructive/non-destructive
Launching - mechanical launching methods: Slip, lift, construction of slipways for end launch and side launch, launching curves, arresting arrangements, tipping, pivoting/plunging, drop off
Other methods of launching: Launching by floating off (building dock, floating dock), launching using air bags
